Next up is boundaries. In pretty much every game you play there's some kind of boundary, so let's talk about how to handle them. PixiJS doesn't have a built-in boundary method, so in this post we borrow a handy `contain` helper and wire it into the bouncing sprite example from earlier.
JavaScript Core (12): How Can Semicolons Be Optional? Meet ASI

How come JavaScript sometimes needs semicolons and sometimes doesn't? That's down to a mechanism called ASI — Automatic Semicolon Insertion. It's handy, but it can also bite you in surprising ways. This post walks through what ASI is, where it silently breaks your code, and the rules you should keep in mind.
What Do You Do When You Realize You're Underskilled After Joining a New Job?

A few people in similar situations have come to chat with me about their recent work. The recurring theme: "After joining I realized my skills can't keep up at all, I can't contribute in meetings — should I just quit?"
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (18) — How boxesIntersect Actually Decides a Collision

Time to circle back to something I glossed over earlier — the parameters inside boxesIntersect. We'll break down what getBounds() returns, then walk through each comparison in that long return statement step by step so you can see exactly when two boxes count as colliding and when they don't.
JavaScript Core Concepts (11): Statements vs Expressions — The One Everybody Mixes Up

Statements vs expressions is one of those concepts almost everyone gets fuzzy on — me included. In this post we untangle the difference: why a variable declaration counts as a statement even though it has an equals sign, why expressions always return a value, and how function declarations, function expressions, blocks and object literals fit into the picture.
JavaScript Core Concepts (10): Execution Context & Scope — Practice Quiz

We've finally reached the last post in the series — a practice quiz. Most of these questions I wrote myself, but they're still well worth running through to lock in everything we've covered. One tip: don't just paste the code into a console and run it. Trace through it in your head first and figure out the answer by reasoning about what should happen.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (17) - Adding Text to Your HP Bar

Last time we built an HP bar, but what about the text on it? In this post we cover the easy part — adding text on top of the bar with PIXI.Text, swapping in a variable for the current HP, and using the game loop (ticker) to drain health and update the number in real time.
JavaScript Core Concepts (9): Threads, Sync, and Async — Why Order Matters

This is where we get into one of the most important ideas in JavaScript — the whole sync vs async, single-threaded thing that trips so many people up. We'll start with what a thread even is, why JavaScript can only do one thing at a time, and how the Event queue lets it handle async work anyway.
How to Set a Dynamic Page Title in Vue
Sometimes you want the page title to change with the route — Home, About, etc. How do you do that in a Vue SPA?
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (16) - Building a Game HP Bar

Starting from the last post, our project finally started to feel a bit like an actual game. So in this one we'll build something every game needs — an HP bar. We'll draw it ourselves with two stacked rounded rectangles and a controllable width property to represent the health percentage.
